based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/external/pytorch.adam.golden.jsonl. v0.9.1 Adam/AdamW adversarial plate — each fixture surfaces a distinct attack class with a deterministic mutation.

mutation.explanation: Per-update arithmetic (m_after, v_after, bias-corrected m_hat/v_hat, update formula) is internally consistent — Rules 20, 22, 23, 24 all pass. Only Rule 14's engine recompute catches the divergence. This load-bearing fixture proves that Adam-rule structural consistency is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT for producer authenticity; engine recompute (Rule 14) is the second line of defense and the Fang et al. 2023 PoL spoofing class (arXiv:2208.03567) maps directly here.

reconciliation_check_targeted_first: Rule 14: Engine recompute differential — Adam-internal arithmetic consistent but weight_after stored value diverges from engine recomputation.
purpose: v0.9.1 Adam/AdamW anti-circularity fixture (kind: 'engine-recompute-disagrees-adam'). Pressure-tests that the named rule fires BEFORE the reconciler consults fixture_status metadata.
v0_9_1_trust_framing: Adam-rule trust framing (load-bearing): Rules 22, 23, 24 are STRUCTURAL CONSISTENCY checks, NOT producer-authenticity checks. An attacker who controls every byte of the sidecar and recomputes a consistent (g, m, v, update) quadruple passes Rules 22-24 trivially — analogous to Rule 17's bundle-integrity caveat and the Fang et al. 2023 EuroS&P spoofing class against Proof-of-Learning (arXiv:2208.03567).
